label: notes
ground_truth: {"gt_parse": {"general": {"text": "quality issues related to the lots and the supply of domestic water to be drawn from wells located on the lots. ALTA CIVILT CONIRACTORS LTD. has agreed that it should be a general and indispensable condition of the sale of all or any of the lots that the several owners thereof should be informed of the potential water quality issues related to the lots as set forth in the water quality advisory forming part of this subdivision plan. The lots are located within on area where the bedrock geology may interact with groundwater to produce elevated concentrations of the elements arsenic, barium, lead and E.coli. As a result, there may be a higher than average probability that groundwater from wells drilled on the lots may have concentrations exceeding the Canadian Drinking Water Quality Guidelines.Consequently, not groundwater drawn from wells located on the lots may be utilized for consumption or cooking prior to testing for the suitability of consumption and such use. In the event that the measured concentration of arsenic and uranium exceeds the Canadian Drinking Water Quality Guidelines, the water drown from wells located on the lots must by propriate means prior to human ingestion. Failing the installation of treated by appropriate means such necessary treatment measures will require that the owners of the lots use bottled water for cooking and drinking."}}}

ground_truth: {"gt_parse": {"general": {"text": "ALTA CIVIL CONTRACTORS LTD. is the owner of the Lots being subdivided as shown on this subdivision plan. ALTA CIVIL CONTRACTORS LTD. wishes to inform the public of the potential water quality issues related to the lots and the supply of domestic water to be drawn from wells located on the lots. ALTA CIVIL CONTRACTORS LTD. has agreed that it should be a general and indispensable condition of the sale of all or any of the lots that the several owners thereof should be informed of the potential water quality issues related to the lots as set forth in the water quality advisory forming part of this subdivision plan. The lots are located within an area where the bedrock geology may interact with groundwater to produce elevated concentrations of the elements arsenic, barium, lead and E.coll. As a result, there may be a higher than average probability that groundwater from wells drilled on the lots may have concentrations exceeding the Canadian Drinking Water Quality Guidelines.Consequently, no groundwater drawn from wells located on the lots may be utilized for consumption or cooking prior to testing for the suitability of consumption and such use. in the event that the measured concentration of arsenic and uranium exceeds the Canadian Drinking Water Quality Guidelines, the water drown from wells located on the lots must by treated by appropriate means prior to human ingestion. Falling the installation of such necessary treatment measures will require that the owners of the lots use bottled water for cooking and drinking, and drinking,"}}}
